mainTopic: (continues) המוכר שדה לחבירו ונמצאת שאינה שלו · אחריות טעות סופר
overview: (1) An extensive debate between Rav and Shmuel regarding whether a buyer of land that turns out not to belong to the seller retains the improvements (שבח) and the principal (מעות), including various scenarios of theft, debt, and the nature of the payment. (2) A discussion on the liability for a scribe's error in documents, leading to a debate on the validity of selling future acquisitions and the concept of 'trust' (הימנותא) in transactions. — discussion continues into next amud.

sugyaBoundaries.topic: (continues) המוכר שדה לחבירו ונמצאת שאינה שלו
sugyaBoundaries.gist: An extensive debate between Rav and Shmuel regarding whether a buyer of land that turns out not to belong to the seller retains the improvements (שבח) and the principal (מעות), including various scenarios of theft, debt, and the nature of the payment.

A discussion on the liability for a scribe's error in documents, leading to a debate on the validity of selling future acquisitions and the concept of 'trust' (הימנותא) in transactions. — discussion continues into next amud.

steps.title: Challenge from Daily Practice
steps.stepSummary: The Gemara challenges the previous resolution by citing daily practice where Shmuel collected even for enhancements reaching the shoulders.
steps.whatsHappening: The גמרא raises a קושיא from מַעֲשִׂים בְּכָל יוֹם. This observed practice contradicts the distinction just proposed.
steps.deeperAnalysis: The evidence shows שמואל actually מַגְבִּי even for enhancements reaching the shoulders. This proves the previous תירוץ fails because the distinction does not match the practical ruling.

phrases.english: And there were daily incidents of this type,

phrases.aramaic: וְקָא מַגְבֵּי שְׁמוּאֵל אֲפִילּוּ בְּשֶׁבַח הַמַּגִּיעַ לִכְתֵפַיִם!
phrases.english: And Shmuel would collect even for enhancement that reaches one’s shoulders!

rashi.hebrew: והא מעשים בכל יום - דיני טורפי מקח באין לפני שמואל ומגבי לבעל חוב כל השבח עם הקרקע ואפילו מגיע לכתפים כל זמן שצריכין לקרקע:
rashi.takeaway: רש״י explains that the daily practice (מעשים בכל יום) refers to cases of טורפי מקח where שמואל collects the entire enhancement (שבח) along with the land for the creditor, even if it reaches the shoulders, as long as the land is still needed.

rashi.hebrew: הא - דקתני נוטל יציאה מבעל חוב דלא מסיק ביה בעל חוב במוכר שיעור ארעא ושבחא הלכך יהיב בעל חוב ללוקח שבחיה ומסליק ליה והאי דנקט למלתיה בלשון יציאה ולא תנא נוטל כנגד החוב מבעל הקרקע והמותר מבע"ח לאשמועינן היא גופא אתא דהיכא דיציאה יתירה על השבח אין לו אלא יציאה שיעור שבח:
rashi.takeaway: רש״י clarifies that the Mishnah's phrasing 'נוטל יציאה מבעל חוב' implies the creditor did not collect the full value of the land and enhancement from the seller, so the buyer recovers the enhancement from the creditor, and the term 'יציאה' teaches that if the cost exceeds the enhancement, the buyer only recovers the enhancement amount.

steps.title: Resolution: Debt Amount vs. Land Value
steps.stepSummary: The Gemara resolves the difficulty by distinguishing between cases where the debt equals the land plus enhancement versus only the land.
steps.whatsHappening: The גמרא resolves the difficulty by analyzing the debt amount relative to the land's value. It distinguishes between two financial scenarios.
steps.deeperAnalysis: If the debt equals the land plus enhancement, the creditor מַסֵּיק בֵּיהּ everything. If the debt equals only the land, the creditor must מְסַלֵּק לֵיהּ the buyer by paying for the enhancement.

keyTerms.meaning: according to the measure of the land and its enhancement

phrases.aramaic: לָא קַשְׁיָא: הָא דְּמַסֵּיק בֵּיהּ כְּשִׁיעוּר אַרְעָא וּשְׁבָחָא.
phrases.english: Not difficult: This is where he collects from it the value of the land and the enhancement.

phrases.aramaic: הָא דְּלָא מַסֵּיק בֵּיהּ אֶלָּא כְּשִׁיעוּר אַרְעָא,
phrases.english: This is where he does not collect from it except the value of the land,
Where he gives him its enhancement and collects it from him.

tosafot.hebrew: הא דלא מסיק אלא שיעור ארעא תימה כיון דלא מסיק אלא - שיעור ארעא אמאי נוטל שבח היתר על היציאה וי"ל דבלאו הכי פריך שפיר הניחא כו' ולפי המסקנא דמוקי באפותיקי א"ש דאע"פ דלא מסיק ביה אלא שיעור ארעא נותן לו היציאה ללוקח והשבח היתר על היציאה לוקח חנם כדין יורד לתוך שדה חבירו שלא ברשות:
tosafot.takeaway: תוספות questions why the creditor must compensate the buyer for the enhancement when the debt only covers the land's value, and answers that since the case is an אפותיקי, the creditor gives the buyer the land's value and keeps the excess enhancement for free, similar to the law of one who enters a neighbor's field without permission.

steps.title: Challenge: Buyer's Right to Dismiss
steps.stepSummary: The Gemara challenges the resolution by asking why the buyer cannot dismiss the creditor with a portion of the land equal to the enhancement.
steps.whatsHappening: The גמרא challenges the resolution by questioning the buyer's ability to dismiss the creditor. It argues the buyer should have partial rights.
steps.deeperAnalysis: If the buyer had זוּזֵי, he could dismiss the creditor from the entire land. Since he lacks money, he should still be able to dismiss the creditor from a גְּרִבָא דְּאַרְעָא equal to the enhancement's value.

phrases.english: It works out well according to the one who says:

phrases.aramaic: אִי אִית לֵיהּ זוּזֵי לְלוֹקֵחַ לָא
phrases.english: If the purchaser has money, he cannot

phrases.aramaic: מָצֵי מְסַלֵּיק לֵיהּ לְבַעַל חוֹב, שַׁפִּיר.
phrases.english: dismiss the creditor — fine.

phrases.aramaic: אֶלָּא לְמַאן דְּאָמַר כִּי אִית לֵיהּ
phrases.english: But according to the one who says that if he has

phrases.aramaic: זוּזֵי לְלוֹקֵחַ מָצֵי מְסַלֵּיק לֵיהּ לְבַעַל
phrases.english: money, the purchaser can dismiss the creditor,
let him say to him:
If I had money,
I would have dismissed you from the entire land,
Now that I do not have money,
give me a se'ah of land (in the land) [in this
land] the measure of my enhancement!

rashi.hebrew: הניחא למאן דאמר כו' - פלוגתא היא בכתובות (דף צא:):
rashi.takeaway: רש״י identifies the underlying dispute as a פלוגתא found in כתובות (דף צא:).

rashi.hebrew: אי אית ליה זוזי ללוקח - לפרוע החוב:
rashi.takeaway: רש״י clarifies that the phrase 'אי אית ליה זוזי ללוקח' refers to the buyer having the funds to repay the debt.

rashi.hebrew: לא מצי מסלק ליה לבע"ח - מן ארעא דבע"ח קדים:
rashi.takeaway: רש״י explains that 'לא מצי מסלק ליה לבע

tosafot.hebrew: הניחא למ"ד כו' - מפורש בהמקבל (לקמן בבא מציעא דף קי:). (ועיין בכתובות דף צא: ד"ה מאי):
tosafot.takeaway: תוספות points out that this dispute is explicitly discussed in the chapter המוקבל (בבא מציעא דף קי:) and references the discussion in כתובות דף צא:.

steps.title: Resolution: Designated Repayment Land
steps.stepSummary: The Gemara resolves the difficulty by stating the case involves land designated for repayment.
steps.whatsHappening: The גמרא resolves the challenge by introducing the concept of אַפּוֹתֵיקֵי. This designation changes the creditor's exclusive rights.
steps.deeperAnalysis: When land is designated for פֵּרָעוֹן, the creditor has exclusive rights to it. The buyer cannot dismiss the creditor from any portion, even with the enhancement's value.

phrases.aramaic: הָכָא בְּמַאי עָסְקִינַן – כְּגוֹן שֶׁעֲשָׂאוֹ אַפּוֹתֵיקֵי.
phrases.english: Here with what are we dealing? — such as that he made it apoteiki.
phrases.notes: Apoteiki refers to land designated as collateral for a debt.

phrases.aramaic: דַּאֲמַר לֵיהּ: לֹא יְהֵא לְךָ פֵּרָעוֹן אֶלָּא מִזּוֹ.
phrases.english: For he said to him: Let there be no repayment for you except from this.

rashi.hebrew: הכא במאי עסקינן - האי ברייתא דלעיל:
rashi.takeaway: רש״י clarifies that the Gemara's resolution applies specifically to the ברייתא discussed earlier in the sugya.

tosafot.hebrew: כגון דשוייא אפותיקי - השתא מצי איירי דמסיק ביה אפי' שיעור ארעא ושבחא ואפ"ה כיון דשוייא אפותיקי צריך ליתן היציאה כדין יורד כו' כדאמרי' בפרק המקבל (שם.) גבי יתומים אומרים אנו השבחנו ורוצים היציא' דמיירי באפותיקי ואע"ג דמסיק בהו שיעור ארעא ושבחא דהא אם אביהם השביח גובה הכל ומיהו אין ראיה דשאני יתומים שאין להם על מי לחזור ליקח היציאה דכיון שעשה אביהם אפותיקי הרי הוא כיורד לתוך שדה חבירו שלא ברשות שאינו מפסיד היציאה אבל בלוקח שאינו מפסיד כלום שהמוכר משלם לו היציאה והשבח שזה פוטרו מחובו מצינו למימר דאפילו באפותיקי כיון דמסיק ביה שיעור ארעא ושבחא שקיל הכל שבשביל שעשאה אפותיקי לא יגרע כחו וא"ת כיון דאמר ליה לא יהא לך פרעון אלא מזו כשמוכר לאחר הוה ליה כלוקח מגזלן שהרי אינו של מוכר א"כ למה נוטל מן המוכר השבח היתר על היציאה והא לוקח מגזלן לשמואל לית ליה שבחא וי"ל כגון שמכרה קודם שבא זמנו של בעל חוב דחל המקח עד הזמן ולא דמי לגזלן שלא חל המקח כלל דזוזי הוו הלואה ואם ישלם שבח הוה ליה רבית ואפילו מכרה אחר הזמן היה סבור לפייס בע"ח בזוזי וחל המקח עד שטרפה ממנו:
tosafot.takeaway: תוספות explains that when land is designated as אפתיקי, the creditor must pay for the יציאה (enhancement) like a יורד לתוך שדה חבירו, drawing a parallel to the case of יתומים in פרק המקבל, while distinguishing the current case of a buyer who can recover costs from the seller.

steps.title: Dispute on Buying Known Stolen Land
steps.stepSummary: The Gemara introduces a new dispute between Rav and Shmuel regarding a buyer who purchases land knowing it is stolen.
steps.whatsHappening: The גמרא transitions to a new topic with an איתמר. It introduces a dispute between רב and שמואל regarding a buyer who recognized the land was stolen.
steps.deeperAnalysis: רב holds the buyer recovers מָעוֹת but not שֶׁבַח. שמואל holds the buyer recovers neither. This sets up the central debate of the sugya.

keyTerms.meaning: recognized that it is not his

phrases.english: He recognized that it was not his and took it,
Rav says: He has money,
He has no enhancement.

phrases.aramaic: וּשְׁמוּאֵל אָמַר: אֲפִילּוּ מָעוֹת אֵין לוֹ.
phrases.english: And Shmuel says: Even money he does not have.

rashi.hebrew: הכיר בה - לוקח בקרקע זו:
rashi.takeaway: רש״י clarifies that the buyer recognized that the land was not the seller's property.

rashi.hebrew: שאינה שלו - של מוכר שגזולה היתה אצלו ולקחה:
rashi.takeaway: רש״י explains that the land was stolen from its original owner and was in the possession of the thief-seller.

rashi.hebrew: מעות יש לו - מן המוכר לכשיוציאנה נגזל מידו:
rashi.takeaway: רש״י states that Rav holds the buyer can recover the purchase price from the seller when the original owner reclaims the land.

rashi.hebrew: שבח אין לו - שקרקע זה אינו קנוי לו:
rashi.takeaway: רש״י explains that Rav denies the buyer any claim to the enhancement because the land was never legally acquired by the buyer.

tosafot.hebrew: מעות יש לו שבח אין לו - תימה בהגוזל קמא (ב"ק דף צה. ושם: ד"ה דלמא) תנא ה' גובין מן המחוררין שבח ומוקי לה בת"ח דידע דקרקע אינה נגזלת אלמא אפילו בהכיר בה יש לו שבח וי"ל דהתם מיירי כשקיבל עליו אחריות:
tosafot.takeaway: תוספות notes a difficulty from בבא קמא where a תלמיד חכם who knew land cannot be stolen still recovers the enhancement, and resolves it by distinguishing that case where the buyer accepted responsibility.

steps.title: Question: Basis of the Dispute
steps.stepSummary: The Gemara asks on what principle Rav and Shmuel disagree.
steps.whatsHappening: The גמרא asks בְּמַאי קָמִיפְּלְגִי. It seeks the underlying principle behind רב and שמואל's disagreement.
steps.deeperAnalysis: The question probes the reasoning regarding the buyer's rights. It prepares the ground for explaining the dispute's foundation.

phrases.english: With regard to what do they disagree? Rav holds:
A person knows that he has no land,
and he resolved and gave [it] for a deposit.
And let him say to him: for a deposit!
He thought [the seller] does not accept.

rashi.hebrew: לא מקבל - לשמור:
rashi.takeaway: רש״י explains that the phrase לא מקבל means the buyer refuses to accept the land as a פיקדון for safekeeping.

steps.title: Answer: Deposit vs. Gift
steps.stepSummary: The Gemara answers that Rav views the money as a deposit and Shmuel views it as a gift.
steps.whatsHappening: The גמרא answers the question by explaining the intent behind the money. It contrasts רב's view of a deposit with שמואל's view of a gift.
steps.deeperAnalysis: רב assumes the buyer intended the money as a פִּקָּדוֹן because he knew the sale was invalid. שמואל assumes the buyer גָּמַר וְנָתַן it as a מַתָּנָה for the same reason.

phrases.english: With regard to what do they disagree? Rav holds:
A person knows that he has no land,
and he resolved to give it as a deposit.
And let him say to him: as a deposit!
He thought he would not accept. And Shmuel holds:
A person knows that he has no land,
and he resolved to give it as a gift.
And let him say to him: as a gift!
It embarrasses him with the matter.

rashi.hebrew: לא מקבל - לשמור:
rashi.takeaway: רש״י explains that the phrase לא מקבל means the seller refuses to accept the money as a deposit for safekeeping.

tosafot.hebrew: ונתן לו לשם מתנה - אבל בערכין פרק המוכר שדה (דף כט: ושם) גבי המוכר שדהו בשנת היובל עצמה דאמר שמואל מעות חוזרין ליכא למימר אדם יודע שאין מכר ביובל דאין זה פשוט דהא אמר רב מכורה ויוצאה:
tosafot.takeaway: תוספות distinguishes this case from the discussion in ערכין regarding a field sold in the Jubilee year, noting that שמואל's principle of the buyer knowing the sale is invalid does not apply there because רב holds the sale is valid until the Jubilee year ends.

steps.title: Proof from betrothing one's sister
steps.stepSummary: The Gemara brings a proof from a case of betrothing one's sister to support the dispute.
steps.whatsHappening: The גמרא cites a parallel case of betrothing one's sister to support the dispute between רב and שמואל. This is a setup line introducing a comparative analogy.
steps.deeperAnalysis: The case mirrors buying stolen land: both involve an invalid transaction where the money's status is disputed. רב views the money as a deposit, while שמואל views it as a gift.

phrases.english: And they disagree about it one time,
As it was stated: One who betroths his sister,
Rav says: The money returns,
And Shmuel says: The money is a gift.
Rav says: The money returns,

phrases.aramaic: אָדָם יוֹדֵעַ שֶׁאֵין קִידּוּשִׁין תּוֹפְסִין בַּאֲחוֹתוֹ,
phrases.english: A person knows that betrothal does not take effect with his sister,
And he resolved and gave for the sake of a deposit.
And let him say to her for the sake of a deposit!
He thought she does not accept from him.
And Shmuel says: The money is a gift –

phrases.aramaic: אָדָם יוֹדֵעַ שֶׁאֵין קִידּוּשִׁין תּוֹפְסִין בַּאֲחוֹתוֹ,
phrases.english: A person knows that betrothal does not take effect with his sister,
And he resolved and gave for the sake of a gift.
And let him say to her for the sake of a gift!
The matter is embarrassing to her.

rashi.hebrew: מעות חוזרין - הקדושין חוזרין לו:
rashi.takeaway: רש״י clarifies that when רב says מָעוֹת חוֹזְרִין, he means the money is returned to the giver because the קידושין are invalid.

steps.title: Resolution: necessity of both cases
steps.stepSummary: The Gemara resolves the apparent redundancy by stating both cases are necessary to prevent misunderstanding.
steps.whatsHappening: The גמרא explains why both cases are necessary despite their similarity. This is a transition line resolving apparent redundancy.
steps.deeperAnalysis: If only one case were presented, one might think the ruling applies only to that specific case. Both cases are needed to establish the general principle.

keyTerms.meaning: let us say he concedes to him

phrases.english: It is necessary: for if it were stated in this case,
in this case Rav says,

phrases.aramaic: דְּלָא עָבְדִי אִינָשֵׁי דְּיָהֲבִי מַתָּנוֹת לְנוּכְרָאָה.
phrases.english: that people do not give gifts to a non-relative.

phrases.aramaic: אֲבָל גַּבֵּי אֲחוֹתוֹ אֵימָא מוֹדֵה לֵיהּ לִשְׁמוּאֵל.
phrases.english: But regarding his sister, perhaps he concedes to Shmuel.
And if it were stated in that case,
in that case Shmuel says:

phrases.aramaic: אֲבָל בְּהָא אֵימָא מוֹדֶה לֵיהּ לְרַב, צְרִיכָא.
phrases.english: But in this case, perhaps he concedes to Rav, it is necessary.

steps.title: Question: how does the buyer justify taking possession?
steps.stepSummary: The Gemara asks how the buyer justifies taking possession of the land and consuming its produce.
steps.whatsHappening: The גמרא raises a question about the buyer's actions despite knowing the sale is invalid. This is a setup line introducing a new inquiry.
steps.deeperAnalysis: If the buyer knows the sale is invalid, why does he take possession and consume the produce? This question seeks to understand the buyer's reasoning.

phrases.english: Between Rav who says deposit,
Between Shmuel who says gift,
This to the land in what is he descending?
phrases.notes: Refers to the purchaser taking possession of the land despite the invalid acquisition.

phrases.english: And its fruits how does he eat?
phrases.notes: Asks how the purchaser justifies consuming the produce.

rashi.hebrew: בין לרב דאמר כו' - כלומר היכי מצינן לומר טעמא דרב משום דגמר ונתן לשום פקדון וטעמא דשמואל משום דגמר ונתן לשום מתנה ואי הוה דעתיה לחד מהנך היכי הוה נחית להאי ארעא לעובדה ולאכול פירותיה על כרחיה סבור היה שיהא המכר קיים ולא היה בקי בדין וטעמא דפלוגתא דרב ושמואל טעם אחרינא הוא:
rashi.takeaway: רש״י explains that the buyer's actions of taking possession and consuming produce prove he believed the sale was valid, meaning the dispute between רב and שמואל regarding פקדון or מתנה must be based on a different rationale.

steps.title: Answer: buyer acts as if he owns it until owner comes
steps.stepSummary: The Gemara answers that the buyer acts as if he owns the land until the true owner comes.
steps.whatsHappening: The גמרא provides an answer to the question about the buyer's actions. This is a transition line resolving the previous inquiry.
steps.deeperAnalysis: The buyer reasons that he will work the land and consume the produce as if he owns it. When the true owner comes, the money will be designated as a deposit or gift according to רב or שמואל.

keyTerms.meaning: I will take possession of the land
when the master of the land comes

phrases.aramaic: סָבַר: אֲנָא אֵיחוֹת לְאַרְעָא וְאֶיעְבֵּיד וְאֵיכוֹל בְּגַוַּיהּ
phrases.english: He thought: I will take possession of the land and work it and eat from it

phrases.aramaic: כִּי הֵיכִי דַּהֲוָה קָא עָבֵיד אִיהוּ,
phrases.english: Just as he was doing,

phrases.aramaic: לְכִי אָתֵי מָרֵיהּ דְּאַרְעָא זוּזַאי נֶהְווֹ,
phrases.english: When the owner of the land comes, the coins will be
According to Rav who says deposit – deposit,
According to Shmuel who says gift – gift.

rashi.hebrew: דהוה עביד - הגזלן:
rashi.takeaway: רש״י clarifies that the phrase כי היכי דהוה עביד אייהו refers to the actions of the גזלן (the thief who originally stole the land).

rashi.hebrew: לרב דאמר פקדון פקדון - כלומר אליבא דרב מפרשינן דהאי מימר אמר זוזי נהוו פקדון ולשמואל מפרשינן דהאי אמר זוזי נהוו מתנה:
rashi.takeaway: רש״י explains that the Gemara's statement about the money becoming a פקדון or מתנה is interpreted according to the respective opinions of רב and שמואל regarding the status of the payment.

steps.title: Rava's final ruling on the dispute
steps.stepSummary: Rava rules that the buyer can recover both the money and the enhancement, even if not explicitly stated, but not if he knew the land was stolen.
steps.whatsHappening: רבה provides the final halachic ruling on the dispute. This is a conclusion line establishing the practical law.
steps.deeperAnalysis: רבה rules in favor of רב regarding the buyer's rights, but distinguishes between cases where the buyer knew the land was stolen and where he did not. If he knew, he can only recover the money, not the enhancement.
steps.whatToRemember: This is the final ruling of the sugya.
steps.whyThisMatters: It establishes the halachic outcome of the dispute.

phrases.english: Rava said: The halakha is:

phrases.aramaic: יֵשׁ לוֹ מָעוֹת וְיֵשׁ לוֹ שֶׁבַח,
phrases.english: He has money and he has enhancement,

phrases.aramaic: וְאַף עַל פִּי שֶׁלֹּא פֵּירֵשׁ לוֹ אֶת הַשֶּׁבַח.
phrases.english: And even though he did not specify to him the enhancement.
He recognized that it is not his and he took it –
He has money,
He does not have enhancement.

rashi.hebrew: הלכתא - בפלוגתא קמייתא יש לו מעות ויש לו שבח:
rashi.takeaway: רש״י explains that רבה's ruling of 'יֵשׁ לוֹ מָעוֹת וְיֵשׁ לוֹ שֶׁבַח' applies specifically to the first dispute discussed in the sugya.

rashi.hebrew: ואע"פ דלא פירש - ולית לן דשמואל דאמר שבח צריך לימלך:
rashi.takeaway: רש״י clarifies that the phrase 'אַף עַל פִּי שֶׁלֹּא פֵּירֵשׁ לוֹ אֶת הַשֶּׁבַח' indicates we do not follow שמואל's opinion that the buyer must be explicitly informed about the enhancement to claim it.

steps.title: Rav's ruling on the retroactive validity of a sale of stolen land
steps.stepSummary: The sugya opens with Rav's ruling that a scribe's omission of the guarantee clause is a scribal error, and Shmuel asks Rav about a robber who sells stolen land and then buys it back from the owner.
steps.whatsHappening: The sugya begins with a ruling by רב regarding the validity of documents, followed by a question from שמואל about a specific case of a robber selling land he doesn't own and then acquiring it. This is a setup line introducing the core issue.
steps.deeperAnalysis: רב establishes that the omission of the guarantee clause is a scribal error, implying the document is still valid. שמואל's question sets up the core issue: does the subsequent acquisition of the land by the robber retroactively validate the initial sale to the third party?

keyTerms.meaning: Guarantee clause omission is a scribal error
All rights that will come into his possession

steps.whatToRemember: The core principle is that the robber sold 'all rights that will come into his possession' (כָּל זְכוּת שֶׁתָּבוֹא לְיָדוֹ).
steps.whyThisMatters: This establishes the legal mechanism of 'future acquisition' (זכות עתידה לבוא) which is central to the entire sugya.

phrases.english: It is a scribal error of omission
Whether in promissory notes
Or in deeds of purchase and sale
Shmuel asked Rav:
He returned and bought it from the first owners
What is the law? He said to him:
What did the first sell to the second –
All rights that will come into his hand

rashi.hebrew: ואחריות טעות סופר הוא - ולית לן דשמואל דאמר שעבוד צריך לימלך:
rashi.takeaway: רש״י explains that Rav's ruling that the omission of אחריות is a טעות סופר implies we do not follow שמואל's opinion that a שעבוד requires explicit deliberation (לימלך).

rashi.hebrew: חזר - גזלן ולקחה מבעלים הראשונים לאחר שמכר ללוקח:
rashi.takeaway: רש״י clarifies that the case involves a גזלן who sold the land and subsequently repurchased it from the original owners.

rashi.hebrew: מהו - להיות במקום בעלים כאילו לקחה אחר ויוציאה מיד הלוקח:
rashi.takeaway: רש״י explains that the question is whether the גזלן, having repurchased the land, can now act as the owner and evict the buyer who purchased from him initially.

rashi.hebrew: אמר ליה - לא מצי לאפוקה שכשמכר מכר לו כל זכות שתבא לידו וכשלקחה לא לקחה אלא כדי שתהא מקוים ביד הלוקח:
rashi.takeaway: רש״י explains Rav's answer that the initial sale transferred all future rights (כל זכות שתבא לידו), so when the גזלן repurchased the land, he only acquired it to fulfill the obligation to the original buyer, thus cannot evict him.

tosafot.hebrew: חזר ולקחה כו' - בלא הכיר בה מיירי וקמיבעיא ליה אם הגזלן יכול לבטל המקח ולהחזיר לו מעותיו או לאו:
tosafot.takeaway: תוספות clarifies that the case assumes the גזלן did not recognize the land when repurchasing it, and the question is whether he can now cancel the initial sale and return the money to the buyer.

steps.title: The motivation for the robber's subsequent purchase
steps.stepSummary: The Gemara asks why the robber would bother buying back the land he already sold, and offers two explanations: to avoid being called a robber or to maintain his reliability.
steps.whatsHappening: The גמרא questions the robber's motivation for repurchasing the land, offering two possible reasons. This is a setup line introducing psychological factors.
steps.deeperAnalysis: מר זוטרא suggests the robber wants to avoid the stigma of being called a 'robber' by the buyer. רב אשי suggests the robber wants to maintain his general reliability as a trustworthy person.

keyTerms.meaning: It is preferable for him not to be called a robber
It is preferable for him to maintain his reliability

phrases.english: What is the reason?

phrases.aramaic: מָר זוּטְרָא אָמַר: נִיחָא לֵיהּ דְּלָא נִקְרְיֵיהּ גַּזְלָנָא.
phrases.english: Mar Zutra said: It is preferable to him that he not be called a robber.

phrases.aramaic: רַב אָשֵׁי אָמַר: נִיחָא לֵיהּ דְּלֵיקוּ בְּהֵמָנוּתֵיהּ.
phrases.english: Rav Ashi said: It is preferable to him that they rely on his reliability.

rashi.hebrew: דלא ליקרייה גזלנא - שלא יחרפנו לוקח זה כשיוציאנה נגזל מידו ויקראנו גזלן לכך חוזר להעמיד ממכרו:
rashi.takeaway: רש״י explains that the robber repurchases the land to prevent the buyer from humiliating him by calling him a גזלן when the original owner reclaims it.

steps.title: Practical difference between the two motivations
steps.stepSummary: The Gemara asks for the practical difference between the two motivations, suggesting a case where the buyer died.
steps.whatsHappening: The גמרא seeks a practical distinction between the two motivations by proposing a scenario where the original buyer has died. This is a setup line introducing a test case.
steps.deeperAnalysis: If the buyer is dead, the 'avoid being called a robber' motivation is moot because there is no one to call him that. However, the 'maintain reliability' motivation still applies because the robber wants to be seen as reliable even towards the buyer's heirs.

keyTerms.meaning: What is the practical difference between them?

phrases.aramaic: מַאי בֵּינַיְיהוּ? אִיכָּא בֵּינַיְיהוּ דְּמִית לוֹקֵחַ:
phrases.english: What is the difference between them?

phrases.aramaic: מַאן דְּאָמַר נִיחָא לֵיהּ דְּלָא לִקְרְיֵיהּ גַּזְלָנָא –
phrases.english: The one who says it is preferable for him that he not be called a robber –
This one died for him. And the one who says:
It is preferable for him that his reliability stand –

phrases.aramaic: בַּהֲדֵי בְּנֵי נָמֵי נִיחָא לֵיהּ דְּלֵיקוּם בְּהֵמָנוּתֵיהּ.
phrases.english: With regard to people, it is preferable for him that his reliability stand.

rashi.hebrew: דמית לוקח - לאחר שלקחה גזלן מן הבעלים והגזלן בא להוציאה מבניו:
rashi.takeaway: רש״י explains that the scenario of the buyer's death occurs after the robber has already acquired the item from the original owner and is now attempting to evict the buyer's heirs.

tosafot.hebrew: דמית לוקח - פרש"י אחר שלקחה ואין נראה דמיד שקנה הגזלן זכה בו לוקח ואטו משום דמית ליה בתר הכי פקע כחו אלא יש לומר דמית קודם שלקחה הגזלן מנגזל (ומית גזלן) צ"ל שהיה חולה או גוסס בשעה שלקחה מנגזל:
tosafot.takeaway: תוספות challenges רש״י's timeline, arguing that the buyer's acquisition should be valid immediately upon the robber's purchase, and instead suggests the buyer died before the robber acquired the item from the original owner.

meforshim.interplaySummary: תוספות disputes רש״י's assumption that the buyer died after the transaction, arguing that the buyer's rights should not be nullified by subsequent death, and proposes the death occurred prior to the robber's acquisition.
